dialogue.utterance: Hello, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What's happening today?
He's got a knife in his chest and he's bleeding from his wrists.
He's alert to verbal stimuli. GCS is 14: eyes open to verbal per ePCR, responds to verbal, with lethargic affect.
Airway is patent, per ePCR.
Respiratory rate is 18, per ePCR. Breathing is regular, unlabored, and with normal depth.
Equal and strong radial pulses per ePCR, skin is warm and clammy with normal color.
There's a pool of blood underneath him, estimated at about 500mLs per ePCR.
Head is normocephalic, pupils are equal at 2mm and track normally. No discharge from EENT. Three 1cm stab wounds to right side of the neck with bleeding controlled per ePCR. No JVD.
Chest rise and fall are normal per ePCR. Breath sounds are clear and equal. Knife is still embedded in the chest.
Knife is embedded just below the xiphoid process and moves with the heartbeat per ePCR. Bleeding is controlled.

No distention, tenderness, or discoloration in the abdomen per ePCR. Bilateral lacerations at the wrists with controlled bleeding. Left side was controlled naturally, right side with pressure dressing.
Blood pressure is now 127 over 75. Pulse is 90. SpO2 is at 92% per ePCR. Glucose was not obtained per ePCR.
At 12:31, BP is 129 over 69, pulse is 102, respiratory rate is 20, SpO2 is 99% per ePCR. EKG shows sinus tachycardia per ePCR.
At 12:35, BP is 129 over 87, pulse is 86, SpO2 is 98% per ePCR. EKG: sinus rhythm.
At 12:40, blood pressure is 159 over 60, respiratory rate is 17, pulse jumps to 114, SpO2 is 98% per ePCR. EKG shows sinus tachycardia. Pain level is 8 per ePCR.
At 12:43, blood pressure is 138 over 84, pulse is 89, SpO2 is 98% per ePCR. EKG: normal sinus rhythm.
Administering 15LPM via non-rebreather mask per ePCR for hypoxemia. His SpO2 improved from 92% to 99%.
Placing the patient on a cardiac monitor with 4-lead ECG per ePCR. Rhythm is normal sinus.
Starting a 16-gauge IV in the left antecubital vein-successful on the first attempt per ePCR.
Capnography results show normocapnia with regular rate per ePCR.

Let's run a pre-arrival report to notify the hospital: 60-year-old male with self-inflicted knife wounds. 16-gauge IV in left antecubital, non-rebreather on 15LPM. Alert to verbal stimuli with a very weak affect.
Ready to move the patient onto the stretcher with two providers using belts. Semi-Fowler's position per ePCR.
Belts are secured. Semi-Fowler's for ambulance transfer.
Transporting emergently to [COMPANY_NAME]. Protocol: injury - general trauma management, Protocol 4-1. Call is for hemorrhage/lacerations.
Care has been transferred to the hospital staff at the emergency department per ePCR. Patient is en route to the ER for further treatment.
Head and neck are normocephalic with no signs of deformity or swelling per ePCR. Three 1cm stab wounds on right side of the neck with controlled bleeding.
Recheck vitals-still 129/69, pulse 102. Sinus tach on EKG per ePCR.
No subcutaneous air or chest wall instability noted per ePCR. Breath sounds are equal and clear at all fields.
Bilateral lacerations present at wrists, no significant range of motion limitation per ePCR. Strength is preserved.
Oxygen therapy has improved hypoxemia per ePCR.
No adventitious sounds or sounds of obstruction noted. Airway is patent as of assessment per ePCR.
Skin is warm and slightly moist. Capillary refill less than 2 seconds, no evident cyanosis per ePCR.
Transporting in semi-Fowler's position to accommodate chest wound stabilization per ePCR.
Respiratory depth is normal, not increased effort per ePCR. Accessory muscles not used.
No signs of foreign body or obstruction within the nasal passages. No active oropharyngeal bleeding per ePCR.
Glucose measurement not possible per ePCR. No glucose levels recorded.
No masses or palpable structures present in the abdomen per ePCR. Bowel sounds not assessed.
IV insertion in left antecubital vein performed successfully per ePCR. Equipment is correctly placed and functional.
Left arm laceration controlled naturally, no difficulty using or moving the limb per ePCR.
Right arm laceration treated with a pressure dressing, no impairments in movement or sensation noted.
No injuries to the left leg, strength and sensation normal per ePCR.
Right leg is intact with no injuries or limitations per ePCR. Normal in all parameters.
Initial EKG shows normal sinus rhythm per ePCR. Follow-up EKGs show sinus tachycardia at later times.
Breathing remains unlabored and in normal depth with stable respiratory rate per ePCR.
No changes noted in breathing pattern or depth during reassessment per ePCR.
Left antecubital IV site remains patent with no signs of infiltration or bleeding per ePCR.
Constant monitoring of radial pulse shows no changes in quality or rate per ePCR. Circulation remains strong throughout assessment.
No significant changes in the patient's GCS. Still oriented to verbal commands with minimal response per ePCR.
Respiratory depth and effort continue to be normal. Equal breath sounds bilaterally per ePCR.
Continuous normocapnic approach is maintained under capnography monitoring per ePCR.
Left wrist laceration with spontaneous control per ePCR.
Stretcher is secure in semi-Fowler's per ePCR for penetrating chest trauma cases.
Safety straps secured and equipment verified prior to transport per ePCR.
Skin turgor is normal without signs of dehydration per ePCR.
Movement initiated, patient is stable for transit per ePCR.
No palpable lumps or masses in any area of the patient's body per ePCR.
GCS remains stable, pupils PERRL. No motor response assessment possible per ePCR.
Unable to assess memory per ePCR. Patient interaction limited due to low affect.
No facial abnormalities noted per ePCR. Visual fields and eye movement are normal.
Final reassessment of breathing shows respiratory rate unchanged at 17 breaths per minute per ePCR.
All primary and secondary assessments completed as per ePCR. No new injuries reported or injury progression.
No signs of tension pneumothorax or pericardial tamponade per ePCR.
Patient secured using two providers, all straps double-checked per ePCR.
No signs of hematoma or edema in the neck region per ePCR. No neck masses detected.
Thoracic movements symmetrical per ePCR. No signs of thoracic instabilities.
Both upper limbs functioning with preserved motor skills per ePCR. No trauma to fingers detected.
Ambulance equipment secured and functional per ePCR.
No signs of vertebral instability or visible injuries along the spinal column per ePCR.
All monitoring tools remain in use and functional during transport per ePCR.
Cycle completed. All findings consistent per ePCR with no changes between assessments.
If there is any significant change during transport, immediate contact with ED will be made per ePCR.
Transport initiated with continuous observation during trip. Stable patient vitally, but still critical per ePCR.
En route with patient in semi-Fowler's and stabilized per ePCR.
Transport initiated at 12:45 per ePCR. Estimated travel time is 15 minutes.
Patient categorized as semi-stable for transport per ePCR.
Interior temperature maintained at 22°C during transport per ePCR policy.
No deviations from baseline conditions per ePCR. Any further status reported at ED.
Final observation before transfer: stable GCS, BP, pulse, and normal respirations per ePCR.
All records verified and sent to ED in pre-arrival report format per ePCR.
Final skin exam completed with no new findings per ePCR.
Stretcher ready for transfer as per directed protocol per ePCR.
SpO2 improved from 92 to 99 with oxygen per ePCR. All monitors remain active.
Full care protocol followed as in the ePCR. No exclusions.
All patient privacy measures followed, no exposure noted per ePCR.
Patient transferred to ER with minimal intervention; all equipment ready for turnover per ePCR.
All data recorded. No additional reports needed at this stage per ePCR.
